# Copilot Instructions

## Build & Test

**SDK tests** (unit tests, no simulator required):
```bash
cd ShikiTokenSDK
swift test
# Run a single test:
swift test --filter ShikiClientTests/testHighlightSendsCorrectRequest
```

**Demo app** (requires Xcode 15+):
```bash
cd SyntaxHighlightApp
xcodegen generate          # regenerates .xcodeproj from project.yml
open SyntaxHighlightApp.xcodeproj
# Then Cmd+R in Xcode with an iOS 16+ simulator
```

> **Never edit `SyntaxHighlightApp.xcodeproj` directly.** The project file is generated by [xcodegen](https://github.com/yonaskolb/XcodeGen) from `SyntaxHighlightApp/project.yml`. All project config changes (targets, settings, dependencies) belong in `project.yml`.

## Architecture

The app sends source code to a remote [Shiki Token Service](https://github.com/hossain-khan/shiki-token-service) (TypeScript/Hono), which tokenizes it using the same TextMate grammar engine as VS Code, and returns colored JSON tokens. The iOS app renders those tokens as styled `AttributedString` text.

```
SyntaxHighlightApp (demo)
    └── ShikiTokenSDK (Swift Package, zero third-party deps)
            └── ShikiClient ──HTTPS──> Shiki Token Service (remote API)
```

**`ShikiTokenSDK/`** — the distributable Swift Package:
- `Client/` — `ShikiClient` (async/await, `Sendable`) + `ShikiError`
- `Models/` — `Codable` request/response structs, `ShikiLanguage`/`ShikiTheme` constants, three token types
- `UI/` — `CodeHighlightView` SwiftUI component + `Color+Hex` extension

**`SyntaxHighlightApp/`** — demo app (MVVM, SwiftUI):
- `Screens/` — one folder per screen (`Home`, `Highlight`, `DualTheme`, `Semantic`, `LanguageExplorer`)
- `Models/` — `LoadingState<T>`, `CodeSamples`
- A single `ShikiClient` is created in `@main` and passed down via initializer injection

## Key Conventions

### Three token types — different endpoints, different data shapes
All token arrays have shape `[[T]]` (outer = lines, inner = tokens per line):
- **`Token`** `{ text, color }` — single hex color, from `POST /highlight`
- **`DualToken`** `{ text, darkColor, lightColor }` — both themes, from `POST /highlight/dual`
- **`SemanticToken`** `{ text, type: TokenType }` — classified by kind, from `POST /highlight/semantic`

`CodeHighlightView` has three corresponding initializers, one per token type.

### Language/theme constants are uninhabited enums used as namespaces
`ShikiLanguage` and `ShikiTheme` are `enum` with no cases — used purely as namespaces for `static let` string constants (e.g. `ShikiLanguage.swift`, `ShikiTheme.githubDark`). Raw strings are also accepted by all request types.

### `LoadingState<T>` for all async ViewModel state
ViewModels use `@Published var state: LoadingState<T>` with four cases: `.idle`, `.loading`, `.loaded(T)`, `.error(Error)`. Always follow this pattern when adding new screens.

### SDK tests use `MockURLProtocol` (URLProtocol injection)
Tests inject a `URLSession` configured with `MockURLProtocol` into `ShikiClient`. Set `MockURLProtocol.handler` in `setUp`/per-test and call `MockURLProtocol.reset()` in `tearDown`. No networking occurs.

### UIKit-only code is guarded
Features using UIKit APIs (`UIPasteboard`, `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator`, `ImageRenderer`) are wrapped in `#if canImport(UIKit)` to preserve macOS compatibility.

### Dual-token dark/light switching requires no re-fetch
`CodeHighlightView` with `DualToken` reads `@Environment(\.colorScheme)` and switches between `darkColor`/`lightColor` reactively — no new API call needed on appearance change.
